name: Login
description: Standalone /login route, exercised UNauthenticated via the Spec CI unauth-context lane (metadata.requiresUnauthenticated). After the Cognito legacy-auth teardown the app has NO local password form: /login renders the Cognito-only AuthForm — three federated sign-in buttons (Google, Microsoft, GitHub) plus a 'Continue with email' button that routes to the Cognito hosted UI's native email/password screen. Every button triggers a full-page redirect to auth.qontinui.io (an external origin Spec CI does not follow), so this spec is STRUCTURAL ONLY: it asserts the four sign-in entry points are present. There is no behavioral sign-in transition because authentication leaves the app origin (the hosted UI owns credentials); the authenticated Spec CI lane establishes its session by seeding a Cognito token directly (api-auth), not by driving this form.

states.name: Cognito sign-in options
states.description: The /login Cognito-only sign-in surface: three federated buttons (Continue with Google / Microsoft / GitHub) and a 'Continue with email' button (hosted-UI native screen). Matched by button text — the AuthForm buttons carry no author-set id attributes.

assertions.description: 'Continue with Google' federated sign-in button is present.
'Continue with Microsoft' federated sign-in button is present.
'Continue with GitHub' federated sign-in button is present.
'Continue with email' button (routes to the Cognito hosted-UI native email/password screen) is present.
